faqs.question: How quickly can you buy my house in Enfield?
faqs.answer: We can complete the purchase of your Enfield property in as little as 7 days, though 14-21 days is more typical depending on the complexity of the transaction. The timeline is largely in your hands — you choose the completion date that works best for your circumstances. Whether your property is in Enfield Town, Edmonton, Palmers Green, Southgate, Winchmore Hill, or Cockfosters, our process is designed for speed without cutting corners on the legal requirements that protect both buyer and seller.

faqs.question: How much will you offer for my Enfield property?
faqs.answer: Our offers are based on detailed analysis of current market conditions in your specific area of Enfield. We typically offer between 75% and 85% of the full open-market value, depending on the property type, condition, and location within the borough. While this is below what you might achieve through a traditional estate agent sale, the absence of agent fees, legal costs, and months of ongoing expenses means the net difference is often much smaller than it appears. We'll explain our valuation methodology transparently so you can make an informed decision.

faqs.question: Do I have to pay any fees when selling to you?
faqs.answer: No, there are absolutely no fees to pay when you sell your Enfield property to us. We cover all legal and conveyancing costs, we pay for our own surveys and assessments, and there are no admin fees, processing charges, or hidden costs of any kind. The cash offer we make is the exact amount you'll receive on completion. This is one of the key advantages over selling through an estate agent, where fees of 1-3% plus VAT can amount to thousands of pounds on a typical Enfield property.

faqs.question: Do you buy properties in any condition?
faqs.answer: Yes, we buy properties in any condition across the London Borough of Enfield. Whether your home is in pristine condition or needs significant renovation — perhaps it's a probate property that hasn't been updated in decades, a rental with tenant wear and tear, or a house with structural issues — we'll make you an offer. We factor the property's condition into our valuation from the outset, so there are no last-minute price reductions following our assessment. You don't need to spend a penny on repairs or cosmetic improvements before selling to us.

faqs.question: What types of property do you buy in Enfield?
faqs.answer: We purchase all types of residential property throughout the London Borough of Enfield. This includes detached houses, semi-detached houses, terraced houses, flats, maisonettes, bungalows, and new-build properties. We buy freehold and leasehold properties, including those with short leases. We also purchase properties with sitting tenants, properties in probate, and homes with various issues that might make them difficult to sell through traditional channels, such as subsidence, non-standard construction, or planning complications.

faqs.question: Can you buy my house if it has a mortgage?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The vast majority of properties we purchase in Enfield have an existing mortgage. On completion, your mortgage is paid off directly from the sale proceeds by your solicitor, and any remaining equity is transferred to you. If your property is in negative equity — where the mortgage exceeds the property's value — we can still discuss your options, though the situation is more complex and may require your lender's consent to a shortfall sale.

faqs.question: How does the cash offer process work?
faqs.answer: The process is straightforward. First, you provide us with details of your Enfield property — the address, type, size, and general condition. Our valuation team then analyses recent sold prices in your area, current market conditions, and your property's specific characteristics. Within 24 hours, we present you with a firm, written cash offer. This offer comes with a clear explanation of how we arrived at the figure, including comparable property data. There's no obligation to accept, and we're happy to answer any questions you might have.

faqs.question: What areas in Enfield do you cover?
faqs.answer: We cover the entire London Borough of Enfield, including Enfield Town (EN1, EN2), Edmonton (N9, N18), Palmers Green (N13), Southgate (N14), Winchmore Hill (N21), Cockfosters (EN4), as well as Oakwood, Grange Park, Bush Hill Park, Ponders End, Enfield Lock, Enfield Highway, and Brimsdown. If your property is within the borough boundaries, we can make you an offer. We also purchase properties in neighbouring boroughs including Barnet, Haringey, and Waltham Forest.

faqs.question: Can I sell my house to you if I'm going through a divorce?
faqs.answer: Yes, we regularly help divorcing couples in Enfield sell their shared property quickly. A cash sale can be particularly beneficial during divorce proceedings because it provides both parties with certainty — you know exactly what you'll receive and when. This can simplify financial settlements and allow both parties to move forward sooner. We can work with both parties' solicitors to ensure the process runs smoothly, and the speed of our service means the emotional strain of maintaining a shared property during proceedings is minimised.

faqs.question: How do I know your offer is fair?
faqs.answer: We encourage you to compare our offer with other valuations before making a decision. Get estate agent appraisals, check recent sold prices in your area through the Land Registry or property portals, and consider obtaining a formal valuation if you wish. Our offer is based on the same comparable data that agents and surveyors use, and we're transparent about our methodology. We also recommend checking our reviews on Trustpilot and Google to see what previous Enfield sellers have said about their experience with us.

faqs.question: Can you buy my property if it's currently tenanted?
faqs.answer: Yes, we purchase properties with sitting tenants across the London Borough of Enfield. This can actually simplify the process, as there's no need to end the tenancy before selling. The existing tenancy agreement transfers to us as the new owner, and the tenant's rights are fully protected. This is particularly useful for landlords looking to exit the buy-to-let market in areas like Edmonton and Palmers Green, where a significant number of properties are privately rented.

faqs.question: What happens after I accept your offer?
faqs.answer: Once you accept our offer, we move quickly. We instruct our solicitors (at our expense) to begin the conveyancing process. We arrange a convenient time to assess your property in person — typically a single visit lasting 30-45 minutes. You choose your preferred completion date, and we work backwards from that to ensure everything is ready on time. Your dedicated case manager keeps you informed at every stage, and you'll have a direct phone line and email for any questions. On completion day, the cash is transferred to your account.

faqs.question: Do I need to have an EPC to sell to you?
faqs.answer: While an EPC is technically required for most property sales, we don't require you to arrange one before we make our offer. If you don't have a valid EPC, we can arrange one on your behalf as part of the sales process, and we cover the cost. This is another way we simplify the selling process for Enfield homeowners — there's no need to spend time or money on preparations before we can proceed with your sale.

faqs.question: Can you help if my house is about to be repossessed?
faqs.answer: Yes, and time is critical in these situations. If you're facing repossession of your Enfield property, selling to a cash buyer can be much faster than the repossession process, potentially saving your credit rating and preserving more of your equity. We've helped numerous homeowners across the borough in this situation, and our ability to complete in as little as 7 days can make the difference between a managed sale and a forced repossession. Contact us immediately on 0800 012 1008 if you're in this situation — early action gives you the most options.

faqs.question: Is the process really as simple as you describe?
faqs.answer: Yes, it genuinely is. We've spent years refining our process to remove every unnecessary complication. You contact us, we make an offer, you choose whether to accept, and we handle everything else. There are no chains, no mortgage applications, no viewings, and no uncertainty. Our solicitors manage the legal work, your case manager coordinates the timeline, and on your chosen completion date, you receive the agreed cash amount. The simplicity is the whole point — we know that Enfield homeowners seeking a quick sale often have enough to deal with without the additional stress of a complicated property transaction.
